sentence_reviews.text: Vectors, matrices, tensor contractions, eigensystems, and linear maps form the numerical language of modern machine learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Appendix A.2 'Vectors, Matrices, and Linear Algebra' (lines 768-774); related application chapters in Parts V-VI.
sentence_reviews.notes: The source directly supports vectors, matrices, and linear algebra as mathematical background, but it does not state the full machine-learning phrasing or the specific inclusion of tensor contractions and eigensystems. This is an atlas synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: Its abstractions require explicit assumptions; a mathematically valid result can still be irrelevant to a mismatched empirical setting.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Linear algebra is modeled as an upstream prerequisite for downstream nodes such as Optimization theory, Numerical analysis, Dynamical systems & control theory.
